content_of_post_to_answer: I keep overthinking every decision and never actually ship anything. How do I break out of the perfectionism loop and start finishing what I start?
answer: Perfectionism is often misunderstood as a desire for excellence; in practice, it is frequently a way of avoiding the moment when your work becomes judgeable. The hidden shift is to stop asking “How do I make this impossible to criticize?” and ask “What can I learn by releasing this version?” Finishing is not the opposite of caring about quality; it is the process that teaches you what quality actually requires. The unfinished idea feels perfect because reality has never tested it.
